title: incarvillea uniflora
intro: Incarvillea uniflora, also known as the Chinese fritillaria or one-flowered incarvillea, is a plant species belonging to the family Bignoniaceae. It is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving coughs, bronchitis, and asthma, as well as treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Incarvillea uniflora often involve drying the plant material to make it suitable for teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
